{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks whether radiotherapy for Hodgkin disease increases the risk of lung cancer. Multiple documents support this connection. Document [19] explicitly states: 'The most common secondary malignancy following treatment of patients with Hodgkin lymphoma is lung cancer.' Document [21] further confirms that 'radiation therapy can also cause primary lung cancer when it is utilized for the treatment of other malignancies such as breast cancer and Hodgkin lymphoma.' Document [12] lists'second primary cancers (lung, thyroid, breast, soft tissue sarcomas)' as complications of radiation therapy. These references directly link radiotherapy for Hodgkin lymphoma to an increased risk of lung cancer. Therefore, the evidence clearly supports that radiotherapy for Hodgkin disease increases the risk of lung cancer.", "answer_choice": "A"}
